---
title: "GO Figure 7"
author: "Elizabeth Campbell"
date: "2026-01-07"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```


```{r}
#===============================================================================
# Gene Ontology Enrichment Visualization - Figure 7
# Temperature vs. Precipitation Pathway Comparison
#
# Author: Elizabeth Campbell
# Affiliation: Department of Tropical Plant and Soil Sciences,
#              University of Hawaii at Manoa
# Date: February 2026
#
# Description: Creates side-by-side comparison of GO enrichment results
#              showing distinct molecular pathways for temperature
#              (hormone signaling) vs. precipitation (chromatin remodeling)
#===============================================================================

library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(patchwork)
library(scales)

# File paths
base_dir <- "C:/Users/Lizzy/OneDrive/Documents/UH_Manoa_Classes/IWG_Chapter_2"
go_dir <- file.path(base_dir, "Data/Gene Ontology")
output_dir <- file.path(go_dir, "Publication_Figures")

setwd(go_dir)
dir.create(output_dir, showWarnings = FALSE, recursive = TRUE)

# Load data
temp_results <- read.csv("GO_enrichment_temperature_group.csv")
precip_results <- read.csv("GO_enrichment_precipitation_group.csv")

cat("Loaded temperature results:", nrow(temp_results), "GO terms\n")
cat("Loaded precipitation results:", nrow(precip_results), "GO terms\n\n")

# Prepare data - top 10 significant terms per category
temp_data <- temp_results %>%
  filter(FDR < 0.05) %>%
  arrange(FDR) %>%
  head(10) %>%
  mutate(
    Term = gsub("\\.\\.\\..*", "", Term),
    Term = reorder(Term, -log10(FDR)),
    Category = "Temperature"
  )

precip_data <- precip_results %>%
  filter(FDR < 0.05) %>%
  arrange(FDR) %>%
  head(10) %>%
  mutate(
    Term = gsub("\\.\\.\\..*", "", Term),
    Term = reorder(Term, -log10(FDR)),
    Category = "Precipitation"
  )

cat("Temperature:", nrow(temp_data), "terms\n")
cat("Precipitation:", nrow(precip_data), "terms\n\n")

#===============================================================================
# Create Publication Figure
#===============================================================================

# Temperature panel
p1 <- ggplot(temp_data, aes(x = -log10(FDR), y = Term)) +
  geom_point(aes(size = Significant), color = "#E69F00", alpha = 0.8) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = -log10(0.05), linetype = "dashed", 
             color = "gray50", linewidth = 0.5) +
  scale_size_continuous(
    name = "Gene\nCount",
    range = c(3, 10),
    breaks = c(3, 6, 9, 12)
  ) +
  scale_x_continuous(
    expand = c(0.02, 0),
    limits = c(1.6, 2.1),
    breaks = seq(1.7, 2.0, 0.1)
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Temperature Adaptation",
    subtitle = "Hormone signaling pathways",
    x = expression(-log[10]*"(FDR)"),
    y = NULL
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 11) +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold", size = 13),
    plot.subtitle = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 10, color = "gray40"),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 10, color = "black"),
    axis.text.x = element_text(size = 9, color = "black"),
    axis.title.x = element_text(size = 11, margin = margin(t = 8)),
    panel.grid.major.y = element_line(color = "gray90", linewidth = 0.3),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    panel.border = element_rect(color = "gray70", fill = NA, linewidth = 0.5),
    legend.position = "right",
    plot.margin = margin(10, 10, 10, 10)
  )

# Precipitation panel
p2 <- ggplot(precip_data, aes(x = -log10(FDR), y = Term)) +
  geom_point(aes(size = Significant), color = "#56B4E9", alpha = 0.8) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = -log10(0.05), linetype = "dashed", 
             color = "gray50", linewidth = 0.5) +
  scale_size_continuous(
    name = "Gene\nCount",
    range = c(3, 10),
    breaks = c(2, 3, 4, 5)
  ) +
  scale_x_continuous(
    expand = c(0.02, 0),
    limits = c(1.6, 2.1),
    breaks = seq(1.7, 2.0, 0.1)
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Precipitation Adaptation",
    subtitle = "Chromatin remodeling pathways",
    x = expression(-log[10]*"(FDR)"),
    y = NULL
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 11) +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold", size = 13),
    plot.subtitle = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 10, color = "gray40"),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 10, color = "black"),
    axis.text.x = element_text(size = 9, color = "black"),
    axis.title.x = element_text(size = 11, margin = margin(t = 8)),
    panel.grid.major.y = element_line(color = "gray90", linewidth = 0.3),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    panel.border = element_rect(color = "gray70", fill = NA, linewidth = 0.5),
    legend.position = "right",
    plot.margin = margin(10, 10, 10, 10)
  )

# Combine panels
combined <- p1 + p2 + 
  plot_annotation(
    tag_levels = 'A',
    theme = theme(plot.tag = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold"))
  )

# Save publication version
ggsave(
  file.path(output_dir, "Figure7_GO_Enrichment_FINAL.png"),
  combined,
  width = 12,
  height = 6,
  dpi = 600,
  bg = "white"
)

ggsave(
  file.path(output_dir, "Figure7_GO_Enrichment_FINAL.pdf"),
  combined,
  width = 12,
  height = 6,
  device = cairo_pdf
)

cat("\n✓ Figure 7 saved successfully!\n")
cat("   - Figure7_GO_Enrichment_FINAL.png\n")
cat("   - Figure7_GO_Enrichment_FINAL.pdf\n\n")

#===============================================================================
# END
#===============================================================================
```
